title: Fix P1: install.ps1 writes BOM-encoded config.json, breaking serde_json parsing
action: Fix a P1 finding from independent review session review-cross-platform-install-20260720 (code-quality reviewer): install.ps1 line 140 currently does `($config | ConvertTo-Json) | Set-Content -Path $ConfigFile -Encoding utf8`. On Windows PowerShell 5.1 (the minimum version this script requires per its #Requires directive, and the default shell an `irm | iex` one-liner runs under), `-Encoding utf8` writes a leading UTF-8 byte-order-mark (EF BB BF). src/config/mod.rs's Config::load_file/load_str calls serde_json::from_str directly with no BOM-stripping, and serde_json treats a leading BOM as invalid JSON (not valid whitespace per the JSON spec) - so the binary fails to parse this installer-written config.json on every launch, meaning the Windows service never starts. Fix by writing BOM-free output: replace the Set-Content line with `[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($ConfigFile, ($config | ConvertTo-Json), (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false)))` (explicit UTF8Encoding with encoderShouldEmitUTF8Identifier=false avoids the BOM regardless of PowerShell version/edition; the config content is pure ASCII today, but this stays correct if a future field ever needs non-ASCII). Do not change anything else in the file - this is a single-line fix.

truths: install.ps1 no longer writes a UTF-8 BOM when creating config.json
The written config.json content (the JSON itself) is otherwise unchanged - same fields, same values, still valid JSON parseable by serde_json::from_str with no leading BOM bytes

artifacts.substantive: the Set-Content -Encoding utf8 call for config.json is replaced with a BOM-free write; nothing else in the file changes

prohibitions: Do not change any other line in install.ps1
Do not change how herdr-go.env / the token file is handled (untouched by this fix)

trace.outcome: Replaced BOM-emitting Set-Content -Encoding utf8 with [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText + UTF8Encoding($false) on install.ps1 line 140 so Windows-installed config.json parses cleanly with serde_json::from_str
